description_long: This is a hand-crafted ceramic vase with a bulbous lower body and a wide, cylindrical neck. The surface is matte and slightly rough, with visible irregularities and subtle undulations, suggesting it was shaped by hand rather than machine. A single, thick handle is attached to one side, curving from the upper neck to the shoulder of the vase, and appears robust but somewhat asymmetrical. The exterior is decorated with faded, abstract blue-gray brushstroke motifs, which are unevenly distributed and partially worn, giving the vase an aged appearance. The rim is thick and uneven, with a slightly wavy edge, and the interior is unglazed, showing a similar rough texture to the outside. Overall, the vase exudes a rustic, utilitarian charm, likely intended for decorative use in an urban outdoor or semi-public setting.
description: A rustic, hand-crafted ceramic vase with a wide neck, thick handle, and faded blue-gray abstract motifs on a matte, rough surface.
description_view_0: The vase is seen from the front, showing the wide neck, thick rim, and the main body with faded blue-gray motifs; the handle is not visible.
description_view_1: This view highlights the thick, curved handle attached to the side, as well as more of the abstract blue-gray patterns on the body.
description_view_2: The back view centers the handle, showing its attachment points and the continuation of the decorative motifs around the body.
description_view_3: The handle is visible from a different angle, and the uneven rim and additional faded motifs on the body are apparent.
attribute_location: urban park bench or public plaza planter
